A ctx-taking provider consumed by a command: the build function's ctx
parameter is the command context, so the provider constructs under the
selected command's cancellation.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli => CLIDIR
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- data/data.go --
package data

import (
	"context"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"
)

type Store struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewStore(ctx context.Context) (*Store, error) { return &Store{}, nil }

//fabrik:cli:command
func Migrate(ctx cli.Context, s *Store) error { return nil }
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"syscall"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/cli"

	"app/data"
)

func run() int {
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
	defer cancel()
	sigc := make(chan os.Signal, 2)
	signal.Notify(sigc, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
	defer signal.Stop(sigc)
	go func() {
		<-sigc
		cancel()
		<-sigc
		os.Exit(1)
	}()

	root := &cli.Command{
		Name: "app",
		Subcommands: []*cli.Command{
			{
				Name: "migrate",
				Run: func(ctx cli.Context) error {
					store, err := buildMigrate(ctx)
					if err != nil {
						return err
					}
					return data.Migrate(ctx, store)
				},
			},
		},
	}
	return root.Exec(os.Args[1:], cli.WithSignalContext(ctx))
}

func buildMigrate(ctx context.Context) (*data.Store, error) {
	// Providers
	dataStore, err := data.NewStore(ctx)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return dataStore, nil
}
